concerns.text: FR-005 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping the lag window {1, 2, 3, 7, 14} and reporting significance rates. Task T023 implements AIC/BIC lag selection (from the conflicting plan) instead of the mandated fixed-sweep. Task T024 mentions Bonferroni but relies on T023's logic. No task explicitly implements the FR-005 fixed-sweep sensitivity analysis.

SC-002 requires measuring statistical validity against a Bonferroni-corrected alpha (α=0.01). Task T027 validates p < 0.01, but since T023/T024 do not implement the required sensitivity sweep (FR-005) or the Bonferroni correction logic (per plan conflict), there is no task ensuring the SC-002 measurement condition is actually met.

FR-002 and US-2 Edge Cases require preserving zero-event days as valid zeros and NOT interpolating them. Task T015 states 'use linear interpolation for nulls' but does not explicitly include the logic to distinguish nulls from zero-event days, risking the interpolation of valid zeros which violates the spec.

Task T024 description references 'p < 0.01 after Bonferroni correction' while the task implementation (T023) and plan explicitly avoid Bonferroni. This creates a stale/conflicting tag in the task description that does not match the implemented logic or the plan's constraints.
